title: Wireshark conversation/endpoint table — 192.168.157.x lab subnet + Microsoft / TikTok / Bing edge resolutions
html:
Heavily OCR-corrupted Wireshark view from the same FINAL_forreal.pcapng — looks like an endpoint/conversation/DNS-resolution table. Visible: the 192.168.157.x VMware NAT lab subnet (.2, .3, .10, .14, .16, .134, .129), and DNS chains for privacy.microsoft.com → privacy.microsoft.com.edgekey.net → akamaiedge.net, analytics.tiktok.com.tiktok.com → ttdnsa.com → bytefcdn.akamai.net, *.azurefd.net → trafficmanager → t-msedge.net, *.live.persion.com.akamai.net, Akamai 23.x.x.x ranges, etc.

TL;DR

  • Same FINAL_forreal.pcapng capture, but viewed at DNS-resolution / endpoint layer — shows the lab guest contacting Microsoft Privacy, TikTok analytics, Bing, Azure FrontDoor, Akamai, and Let's Encrypt CRL endpoints.
  • The lab subnet (192.168.157.0/24) is the standard VMware NAT range — confirms isolated VM forensics setup.
  • TikTok analytics + Microsoft telemetry + Bing in the same trace = realistic Windows-on-Edge desktop noise; the analyst's job is again to spot the anomaly.

wireshark · packet capture · network forensics · malware analysis lab · osu
